meaning: 鍩哄厠鎷夎开缇ゅ矝[甯岃厞](鍦ㄧ埍鐞存捣鍗楅儴)
examples: The Cyclades islands include tow of Greece's most popular, Santorini and Mykonos.
The Cyclades and archeological Museum is also an allegory to the Mythology of Greece.
An island of southeast Greece in the southern Aegean Sea. It is the smallest of the Cyclades Islands and was traditionally considered sacred to Apollo.
Although Tsakonas, a native Athenian, had visited and sailed the Cyclades as a youth with his family, he didn't have special ties to the island.
